sources.notes: Statement and constraints: count arrays satisfying the cute interval/maximum condition modulo 998244353.
Used to confirm the interval characterization, monotonic-stack reconstruction, and DP with prefix-sum optimization.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate Luogu solution page; not used as evidence in this review.

annotation.summary: Count arrays compatible with a set of cute constraints, where each constraint imposes a maximum/nearest-greater style interval condition.
annotation.constraints: The input size requires an O(n^2) or optimized DP after deriving the structural constraints; answers are modulo 998244353.
annotation.core_idea: Each fixed value determines a valid interval in which it can be the controlling maximum; the constraints must form a non-crossing/laminar structure. A monotonic stack captures exactly the nearest greater boundaries and validates that the imposed intervals do not cross. After sorting/organizing these intervals, count possible arrays by dynamic programming over prefix length and stack/active-interval states. Prefix sums optimize transitions that choose where the next interval/value is placed.
annotation.complexity: O(n^2) after preprocessing in the intended DP formulation.
tricks: turn local cute constraints into non-crossing intervals
use a monotonic stack to recover nearest greater boundaries
optimize counting transitions with prefix sums

solution_variants.name: laminar-interval-stack-dp
solution_variants.summary: Validate the interval structure with a monotonic stack, then count legal completions with DP over the induced laminar order using prefix sums.

tags.evidence: The structural validation relies on nearest-greater boundaries, which are computed and checked by a monotonic stack.
After deriving the laminar interval structure, the number of arrays is computed by dynamic programming.
The problem is a counting problem over all arrays satisfying the structural constraints.
DP transitions over possible previous positions are accelerated by prefix sums.
The valid constraint intervals must be nested or disjoint; crossing intervals invalidate the structure.
